boundary/BoundaryEffect.description: An effect that crosses the kernel/external boundary. Specializes effect:ExternalEffect with explicit source or sink binding.
boundary/BoundaryEffect.title: BoundaryEffect

boundary/BoundaryProtocol.description: A specification of the data shape, ordering, and framing constraints for data crossing a boundary.
boundary/BoundaryProtocol.title: BoundaryProtocol

boundary/BoundarySession.description: A Session that includes BoundaryEffects. Extends the session model to track which boundaries were crossed.
boundary/BoundarySession.title: BoundarySession

boundary/EmitEffect.description: A BoundaryEffect that writes a ring datum to a Sink.
boundary/EmitEffect.title: EmitEffect

boundary/IOBoundary.description: A typed interface point between the kernel and the external world. Every data flow into or out of the ring crosses exactly one IOBoundary.
boundary/IOBoundary.title: IOBoundary

boundary/IngestEffect.description: A BoundaryEffect that reads from a Source and produces a datum in the ring.
boundary/IngestEffect.title: IngestEffect

boundary/Sink.description: A typed destination for data leaving the ring. Carries an expected TypeDefinition describing the shape of outgoing data.
boundary/Sink.title: Sink

boundary/Source.description: A typed source of external data entering the ring. Carries an expected TypeDefinition describing the shape of incoming data.
boundary/Source.title: Source

carry/CarryChain.description: The Boolean function chain c_{k+1} = or(and(x_k, y_k), and(xor(x_k, y_k), c_k)). The carry chain is the algebraic mechanism behind the incompatibility metric d_Δ.
carry/CarryChain.title: CarryChain

carry/CarryDepthObservable.description: Observes the carry depth of a Datum in the W₂ tower, computed as the maximum carry-chain length in any operation producing it. Used as the bound observable for the carryConstraintKind BoundConstraint.
carry/CarryDepthObservable.title: CarryDepthObservable

carry/CarryEvent.description: A single carry event at site k. Three kinds: Generate (and(x_k, y_k) = 1), Propagate (xor(x_k, y_k) = 1 and c_k = 1), Kill (neither generate nor propagate).
carry/CarryEvent.title: CarryEvent

carry/CarryProfile.description: The complete carry pattern for an addition x + y. Aggregates carry events across all sites into counts and position masks.
carry/CarryProfile.title: CarryProfile

carry/EncodingConfiguration.description: A mapping from a finite symbol set S to Z/(2^k)Z where 2^k ≥ |S|. Determines how domain values are represented as ring elements.
carry/EncodingConfiguration.title: EncodingConfiguration

carry/EncodingQuality.description: The d_Δ quality metric for an encoding over observed data. Measures how well an encoding minimizes carry-induced metric incompatibility.
carry/EncodingQuality.title: EncodingQuality

cert/BornRuleVerification.description: A certificate attesting that a MeasurementEvent outcome probability matches the Born rule: P(outcome k) = |α_k|² (QM_5). Linked from MeasurementCertificate to provide probability distribution verification.
cert/BornRuleVerification.title: BornRuleVerification

cert/Certificate.description: A kernel-produced attestation. The root class for all certificate types.
cert/Certificate.title: Certificate

cert/ChainAuditTrail.description: An ordered collection of per-step evidence records for a LiftChainCertificate.
cert/ChainAuditTrail.title: ChainAuditTrail

cert/CompletenessAuditTrail.description: An ordered collection of CompletenessWitness records belonging to a CompletenessCertificate. Provides full provenance of the certification process: every constraint applied, every site closed, in sequence.
cert/CompletenessAuditTrail.title: CompletenessAuditTrail

cert/CompletenessCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a type:CompleteType satisfies IT_7d: its constraint nerve has χ = n and all Betti numbers β_k = 0. Issued by the kernel after running the full ψ pipeline on the type's constraint set.
cert/CompletenessCertificate.title: CompletenessCertificate

cert/GenericImpossibilityCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a resolver's verdict path produced a generic impossibility witness — the input failed the resolver's admissibility precondition or decision procedure in a way not covered by a more-specific witness. Returned by every Phase D resolver on failure.
cert/GenericImpossibilityCertificate.title: GenericImpossibilityCertificate

cert/GeodesicCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a trace:GeodesicTrace satisfies both GD_1 conditions: AR_1-ordered and DC_10-selected. Transforms ComputationTrace from descriptive to normative.
cert/GeodesicCertificate.title: GeodesicCertificate

cert/GeodesicEvidenceBundle.description: A structured evidence bundle attesting that each sub-predicate of the geodesic condition (GD_6) holds independently: isAR1Ordered and isDC10Selected. Linked from GeodesicCertificate via evidenceBundle.
cert/GeodesicEvidenceBundle.title: GeodesicEvidenceBundle

cert/GroundingCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a state:GroundedContext has reached full saturation (σ = 1, freeRank = 0, S = 0, T_ctx = 0) per SC_4. The session-layer dual of CompletenessCertificate.
cert/GroundingCertificate.title: GroundingCertificate

cert/InhabitanceCertificate.description: A ComputationCertificate verdict primitive that decides carrier non-emptiness on a type:ConstrainedType. Distinct from cert:CompletenessCertificate, which decides freeRank = 0 on the minimal basis. For ConstrainedType instances admitting multiple satisfying value tuples, InhabitanceCertificate.verified may be true while CompletenessCertificate.verified is false.
cert/InhabitanceCertificate.title: InhabitanceCertificate

cert/InhabitanceImpossibilityCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that the inhabitance decider concluded the input is unsatisfiable — there is no value in the carrier that satisfies the declared constraint conjunction. Returned by `resolver::inhabitance::certify` on failure.
cert/InhabitanceImpossibilityCertificate.title: InhabitanceImpossibilityCertificate

cert/InvolutionCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that an operation is an involution: f(f(x)) = x for all x in R_n.
cert/InvolutionCertificate.title: InvolutionCertificate

cert/IsometryCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a morphism:Isometry preserves metric distances. Certifies the transform is a metric isometry with respect to the specified metric.
cert/IsometryCertificate.title: IsometryCertificate

cert/LiftChainCertificate.description: A kernel-issued certificate attesting that a LiftChain from liftSourceLevel to liftTargetLevel is complete.
cert/LiftChainCertificate.title: LiftChainCertificate

cert/MeasurementCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a trace:MeasurementEvent respected the von Neumann–Landauer bridge (QM_1): preCollapseEntropy = postCollapseLandauerCost at β* = ln 2.
cert/MeasurementCertificate.title: MeasurementCertificate

cert/MultiplicationCertificate.description: A certificate attesting the cost-optimal Toom-Cook splitting factor R for a Datum<L> × Datum<L> multiplication at a given call-site context (stack budget, const-eval regime). Carries the chosen splitting factor, the recursive sub-multiplication count, and the accumulated Landauer cost in nats (priced per op:OA_5). Produced by resolver:MultiplicationResolver.
cert/MultiplicationCertificate.title: MultiplicationCertificate

cert/PartitionCertificate.description: A certificate attesting the partition component classification of a Datum, assigning it to one of Irreducible, Reducible, Units, or Exterior via the partition:PartitionComponent enumeration. Produced by the bridge partition walk during grounding.
cert/PartitionCertificate.title: PartitionCertificate

cert/TransformCertificate.description: A certificate attesting to the properties of a morphism:Transform. Certifies that the transform maps source to target correctly.
cert/TransformCertificate.title: TransformCertificate

cohomology/CoboundaryOperator.description: The coboundary operator δ^k: C^k → C^{k+1}. Satisfies δ² = 0.
cohomology/CoboundaryOperator.title: CoboundaryOperator

cohomology/CochainComplex.description: A cochain complex: a sequence of cochain groups connected by coboundary operators.
cohomology/CochainComplex.title: CochainComplex

cohomology/CochainGroup.description: A cochain group: the dual of a chain group, maps chains to coefficients.
cohomology/CochainGroup.title: CochainGroup

cohomology/CohomologyGroup.description: The k-th cohomology group H^k = ker(δ^k) / im(δ^{k-1}). Measures k-dimensional obstructions.
cohomology/CohomologyGroup.title: CohomologyGroup

cohomology/GluingObstruction.description: A gluing obstruction: a cohomology class that detects when local sections fail to glue.
cohomology/GluingObstruction.title: GluingObstruction

cohomology/LocalSection.description: A local section: a consistent choice of data over a subcomplex.
cohomology/LocalSection.title: LocalSection

cohomology/RestrictionMap.description: A restriction map ρ_{σ,τ}: maps data from a simplex to a face.
cohomology/RestrictionMap.title: RestrictionMap

cohomology/Section.description: A global section of a sheaf: a consistent choice of local data across all simplices.
cohomology/Section.title: Section

cohomology/Sheaf.description: A sheaf F over a simplicial complex: assigns data to each simplex with restriction maps.
cohomology/Sheaf.title: Sheaf

cohomology/Stalk.description: A stalk F_σ: the local data of a sheaf at a simplex σ.
cohomology/Stalk.title: Stalk

conformance/CompileUnitBuilder.description: Builder for CompileUnit admission. Collects rootTerm, quantumLevelCeiling, thermodynamicBudget, and targetDomains. Validates against CompileUnitShape.
conformance/CompileUnitBuilder.title: CompileUnitBuilder

conformance/DispatchDeclaration.description: Builder for DispatchShape. Collects predicate, target resolver, and dispatch priority.
conformance/DispatchDeclaration.title: DispatchDeclaration

conformance/DispatchShape.description: Shape for declaring a new DispatchRule in a DispatchTable.
conformance/DispatchShape.title: DispatchShape

conformance/EffectDeclaration.description: Builder for EffectShape. Collects effect name, target sites, budget delta, and commutation flag.
conformance/EffectDeclaration.title: EffectDeclaration

conformance/GroundedCoordinate.description: Boundary crossing intermediate for a single grounded coordinate value. Not a WitnessDatum — must be validated and minted by the foundation.
conformance/GroundedCoordinate.title: GroundedCoordinate

conformance/GroundedTuple.description: Boundary crossing intermediate for a fixed-size array of GroundedCoordinate values. Stack-resident, no heap allocation.
conformance/GroundedTuple.title: GroundedTuple

conformance/GroundedValueMarker.description: Sealed marker trait class. Implemented only for GroundedCoordinate and GroundedTuple. Prevents downstream crates from substituting arbitrary types.
conformance/GroundedValueMarker.title: GroundedValueMarker

conformance/GroundingDeclaration.description: Builder for GroundingShape. Collects source type, ring mapping, and invertibility contract.
conformance/GroundingDeclaration.title: GroundingDeclaration

conformance/GroundingShape.description: Shape for declaring a GroundingMap from surface data to the ring.
conformance/GroundingShape.title: GroundingShape

conformance/InteractionShape.description: Shape describing the required surface of an InteractionDeclaration consumed by the foundation's InteractionDeclarationBuilder: peer protocol, convergence predicate, and commutator state class. Rejects builders missing any of the three.
conformance/InteractionShape.title: InteractionShape

conformance/LeaseDeclaration.description: Builder for LeaseShape. Collects linear site and lease scope.
conformance/LeaseDeclaration.title: LeaseDeclaration

conformance/LeaseShape.description: Shape for declaring a Lease with LinearSite allocation.
conformance/LeaseShape.title: LeaseShape

conformance/MintingSession.description: Boundary session state tracker. Records crossing count and idempotency flag for the two-phase minting boundary.
conformance/MintingSession.title: MintingSession

conformance/ParallelDeclaration.description: Builder for ParallelShape. Collects site partition and disjointness witness.
conformance/ParallelDeclaration.title: ParallelDeclaration

conformance/PredicateDeclaration.description: Builder for PredicateShape. Collects input type, evaluator term, and termination witness.
conformance/PredicateDeclaration.title: PredicateDeclaration

conformance/PredicateShape.description: Shape for user-declared predicates. Requires a bounded evaluator (termination witness) and input type declaration.
conformance/PredicateShape.title: PredicateShape

conformance/PreludeExport.description: An ontology fact recording that a particular OWL class should appear in the foundation crate's `prelude` module re-exports. The v0.2.1 Rust codegen walks PreludeExport individuals filtered by exportsClass to assemble the prelude membership list.
conformance/PreludeExport.title: PreludeExport

conformance/PropertyConstraint.description: A single required property within a shape: the property URI, its expected range, minimum and maximum cardinality.
conformance/PropertyConstraint.title: PropertyConstraint

conformance/Shape.description: A constraint shape that a Prism-declared extension must satisfy. Analogous to sh:NodeShape in SHACL.
conformance/Shape.title: Shape

conformance/ShapeViolationReport.description: Structured violation diagnostic carrying the shape IRI, constraint IRI, property IRI, expected range, cardinality bounds, and violation kind.
conformance/ShapeViolationReport.title: ShapeViolationReport

conformance/StreamDeclaration.description: Builder for StreamShape. Collects unfold seed, step term, and productivity witness.
conformance/StreamDeclaration.title: StreamDeclaration

conformance/StreamShape.description: Shape for declaring a ProductiveStream (targets stream:Unfold, the coinductive constructor).
conformance/StreamShape.title: StreamShape

conformance/ValidatedWrapper.description: Generic validation-proof wrapper. Proves that the inner value was produced by the conformance checker, not fabricated by Prism code.
conformance/ValidatedWrapper.title: ValidatedWrapper

conformance/ValidationResult.description: The result of validating an extension against a shape: conforms (boolean), and violation details if non-conformant.
conformance/ValidationResult.title: ValidationResult

conformance/ViolationKind.description: The kind of shape violation: Missing, TypeMismatch, CardinalityViolation, ValueCheck, or LevelMismatch.
conformance/ViolationKind.title: ViolationKind

conformance/WitnessDatum.description: Opaque ring element witness. Cannot be constructed outside the foundation crate — only produced by reduction evaluation or the two-phase minting boundary.
conformance/WitnessDatum.title: WitnessDatum

conformance/WitnessDerivation.description: Opaque derivation trace that can only be extended by the rewrite engine. Records rewrite step count and root term content address.
conformance/WitnessDerivation.title: WitnessDerivation

conformance/WitnessSiteBudget.description: Opaque site budget that can only be decremented by PinningEffect and incremented by UnbindingEffect — never by direct mutation.
conformance/WitnessSiteBudget.title: WitnessSiteBudget

conformance/WittLevelDeclaration.description: Builder for WittLevelShape. Collects declared bit width, cycle size, and predecessor level.
conformance/WittLevelDeclaration.title: WittLevelDeclaration

conformance/WittLevelShape.description: Shape for declaring a new WittLevel beyond Q3.
conformance/WittLevelShape.title: WittLevelShape

convergence/AssociativeSubalgebra.description: The subspace H ⊂ O selected when triple interaction converges.
convergence/AssociativeSubalgebra.title: AssociativeSubalgebra

convergence/CommutativeSubspace.description: The subspace U(1) ⊂ SU(2) selected when pairwise interaction converges.
convergence/CommutativeSubspace.title: CommutativeSubspace

convergence/ConvergenceLevel.description: A level in the convergence tower. Four instances: R (dim 1), C (dim 2), H (dim 4), O (dim 8).
convergence/ConvergenceLevel.title: ConvergenceLevel

convergence/ConvergenceResidual.description: The unresolved structure at a convergence level. The β_{2^k−1} = 1 Betti number that persists.
convergence/ConvergenceResidual.title: ConvergenceResidual

convergence/HopfFiber.description: The fiber of the Hopf fibration at a convergence level. Four instances: S⁰, S¹, S³, S⁷.
convergence/HopfFiber.title: HopfFiber

derivation/Derivation.description: A complete term rewriting witness: the full sequence of rewrite steps transforming an original term into its canonical form.
derivation/Derivation.title: Derivation

derivation/DerivationDepthObservable.description: Observes the derivation depth of a Datum, computed as the maximum nesting level of derivation:RewriteStep applications producing it. Used as the bound observable for the depthConstraintKind BoundConstraint.
derivation/DerivationDepthObservable.title: DerivationDepthObservable

derivation/DerivationStep.description: An abstract step in a derivation. Concrete subclasses are RewriteStep (term-level rewriting) and RefinementStep (type-level refinement).
derivation/DerivationStep.title: DerivationStep

derivation/DerivationTrace.description: An ordered sequence of derivation:RewriteStep events produced by replaying a Derivation. Used by uor-foundation-verify to re-derive a certificate from a content-addressed trace without running the deciders. The traceEventCount property records the trace length.
derivation/DerivationTrace.title: DerivationTrace

derivation/InhabitanceCheckpoint.description: A peer of derivation:SynthesisCheckpoint specialised to inhabitance search. Marks an audit point where the resolver state can be restored if the search backtracks.
derivation/InhabitanceCheckpoint.title: InhabitanceCheckpoint

derivation/InhabitanceStep.description: A peer of derivation:SynthesisStep specialised to inhabitance search. Each step represents one navigation in the constraint nerve, either pinning a site to a value or confirming that a predicate evaluates true on the current partial assignment.
derivation/InhabitanceStep.title: InhabitanceStep

derivation/RefinementStep.description: A type-level refinement step: the application of a constraint to narrow a type, pinning additional site coordinates. Complements RewriteStep (term-level) in the derivation hierarchy.
derivation/RefinementStep.title: RefinementStep

derivation/RewriteRule.description: A named rewrite rule that can be applied in a derivation step. Each RewriteRule individual represents a specific algebraic law or normalization strategy used during term rewriting.
derivation/RewriteRule.title: RewriteRule

derivation/RewriteStep.description: A single rewrite step in a derivation: the application of one rewrite rule to transform a term.
derivation/RewriteStep.title: RewriteStep

derivation/SynthesisCheckpoint.description: A persistent snapshot of a ConstraintSearchState at a specific SynthesisStep, allowing a TypeSynthesisResolver to resume exploration after interruption. Essential at Q1+ scale where exhaustive synthesis is computationally significant.
derivation/SynthesisCheckpoint.title: SynthesisCheckpoint

derivation/SynthesisStep.description: A single step in the construction of a SynthesizedType: one constraint added to the synthesis candidate and the resulting change in the constraint nerve's topological signature. Ordered by derivation:stepIndex. Analogous to derivation:RewriteStep in the forward pipeline.
derivation/SynthesisStep.title: SynthesisStep

derivation/TermMetrics.description: Metrics describing the size and complexity of a term.
derivation/TermMetrics.title: TermMetrics

division/AlgebraAssociator.description: The associator [a,b,c] = (ab)c − a(bc). Zero for R, C, H; non-zero for O.
division/AlgebraAssociator.title: AlgebraAssociator

division/AlgebraCommutator.description: The commutator [a,b] = ab − ba. Zero for R and C; non-zero for H and O.
division/AlgebraCommutator.title: AlgebraCommutator

division/CayleyDicksonConstruction.description: The doubling construction that builds each division algebra from the previous: R → C → H → O.
division/CayleyDicksonConstruction.title: CayleyDicksonConstruction

division/MultiplicationTable.description: The explicit product rules for a division algebra’s basis elements.
division/MultiplicationTable.title: MultiplicationTable

division/NormedDivisionAlgebra.description: An algebra over R that is a division ring with multiplicative norm. Exactly four exist (Hurwitz theorem): R, C, H, O.
division/NormedDivisionAlgebra.title: NormedDivisionAlgebra

effect/CompositeEffect.description: An ordered sequence of effects applied atomically. The composition E₁ ; E₂ applies E₁ then E₂.
effect/CompositeEffect.title: CompositeEffect

effect/DisjointnessWitness.description: A certificate that two EffectTargets have empty intersection, enabling commutative reordering.
effect/DisjointnessWitness.title: DisjointnessWitness

effect/Effect.description: A typed endomorphism on state:Context. Maps one site-budget configuration to another. The atomic unit of state mutation in the kernel.
effect/Effect.title: Effect

effect/EffectTarget.description: The set of site coordinates that an effect reads or writes. Determines commutation.
effect/EffectTarget.title: EffectTarget

effect/ExternalEffect.description: An opaque effect declared by a Prism implementation. The kernel treats it as a site-budget transformation satisfying the declared commutation contract. Must carry an effect:externalEffectShape linking to a conformance:EffectShape.
effect/ExternalEffect.title: ExternalEffect

effect/PhaseEffect.description: Rotates the reduction phase angle by Ω^k. Does not alter the site budget. The effect produced by reduction step transitions.
effect/PhaseEffect.title: PhaseEffect

effect/PinningEffect.description: Pins a single free site to a definite value. Decrements freeRank by exactly 1. The effect produced by constraint resolution.
effect/PinningEffect.title: PinningEffect

effect/ReversibleEffect.description: An effect that has a well-defined inverse. PinningEffect and PhaseEffect are reversible; ExternalEffect and CompositeEffect are not in general.
effect/ReversibleEffect.title: ReversibleEffect

effect/UnbindingEffect.description: Releases a pinned site back to free state. Increments freeRank by exactly 1. The effect produced by session boundary reset.
effect/UnbindingEffect.title: UnbindingEffect

failure/ComputationResult.description: The outcome of a computation that may fail: either a Success carrying a datum, or a Failure carrying a typed reason. The coproduct of the success and failure cases.
failure/ComputationResult.title: ComputationResult

failure/ConstraintContradiction.description: Two constraints in the type’s constraint set are jointly unsatisfiable.
failure/ConstraintContradiction.title: ConstraintContradiction

failure/Failure.description: A computation that could not reach convergence. Carries a typed FailureReason and the reduction state at the point of failure.
failure/Failure.title: Failure

failure/FailurePropagation.description: The rule for how failures compose under monoidal and parallel products.
failure/FailurePropagation.title: FailurePropagation

failure/FailureReason.description: A typed classification of why a computation failed.
failure/FailureReason.title: FailureReason

failure/GuardFailure.description: A reduction step guard evaluated to false and no alternative transition exists.
failure/GuardFailure.title: GuardFailure

failure/LiftObstructionFailure.description: A WittLift encountered a non-trivial obstruction that could not be resolved.
failure/LiftObstructionFailure.title: LiftObstructionFailure

failure/PartialComputation.description: A computation that produces a ComputationResult rather than a guaranteed datum. The general case of all computations.
failure/PartialComputation.title: PartialComputation

failure/Recovery.description: A strategy for converting a Failure into a Success by modifying the computation path.
failure/Recovery.title: Recovery

failure/SiteExhaustion.description: The linear budget was exhausted before resolution completed.
failure/SiteExhaustion.title: SiteExhaustion

failure/Success.description: A computation that reached convergence and produced a valid datum. Carries the output datum and the computation certificate.
failure/Success.title: Success

failure/TotalComputation.description: A computation where the FailureReason type is empty — failure is structurally impossible.
failure/TotalComputation.title: TotalComputation

foundation/LayoutInvariant.description: A foundation-level layout invariant. Each instance describes an arithmetic or encoding identity that the foundation's mint primitives and validate_const() pass enforce at compile time, distinct from the ontology-level theorem individuals carried by the op namespace. Violations produce GenericImpossibilityWitness citations against the specific LayoutInvariant IRI, letting consumers distinguish a layout-level failure from a theorem-level failure.
foundation/LayoutInvariant.title: LayoutInvariant

homology/BoundaryOperator.description: The boundary operator ∂_k: C_k → C_{k-1}. Satisfies ∂² = 0.
homology/BoundaryOperator.title: BoundaryOperator

homology/ChainComplex.description: A chain complex: a sequence of chain groups connected by boundary operators.
homology/ChainComplex.title: ChainComplex

homology/ChainFunctor.description: The chain functor C: maps a simplicial complex to a chain complex.
homology/ChainFunctor.title: ChainFunctor

homology/ChainGroup.description: A free abelian group generated by k-simplices (the k-th chain group C_k).
homology/ChainGroup.title: ChainGroup

homology/DeformationComplex.description: The deformation complex of a CompleteType T: a chain complex whose H⁰ = automorphisms, H¹ = first-order deformations, H² = obstructions to extending deformations.
homology/DeformationComplex.title: DeformationComplex

homology/FaceMap.description: A face map d_i: removes vertex i from a simplex, producing a face.
homology/FaceMap.title: FaceMap

homology/HomologyGroup.description: The k-th homology group H_k = ker(∂_k) / im(∂_{k+1}). Measures k-dimensional holes.
homology/HomologyGroup.title: HomologyGroup

homology/HornFiller.description: A witness that a given horn (an incomplete simplex boundary) can be filled, certifying the Kan condition at a specific dimension and position.
homology/HornFiller.title: HornFiller

homology/KInvariant.description: The k-invariant κk that classifies the extension from the (k−1)-truncation to the k-truncation of the Postnikov tower. Trivial κk means the truncation splits as a product.
homology/KInvariant.title: KInvariant

homology/KanComplex.description: A simplicial set satisfying the Kan extension condition. The constraint nerve, when promoted from a SimplicialComplex to a KanComplex, carries a full homotopy type — not just its homology groups.
homology/KanComplex.title: KanComplex

homology/NerveFunctor.description: The nerve functor N: maps a set of constraints to a simplicial complex.
homology/NerveFunctor.title: NerveFunctor

homology/PostnikovTruncation.description: The k-th Postnikov truncation τ≤k of the constraint nerve: a KanComplex whose homotopy groups πj vanish for j > k.
homology/PostnikovTruncation.title: PostnikovTruncation

homology/Simplex.description: A k-simplex: a finite set of k+1 vertices drawn from constraint objects.
homology/Simplex.title: Simplex

homology/SimplicialComplex.description: A simplicial complex: a set of simplices closed under taking faces.
homology/SimplicialComplex.title: SimplicialComplex

interaction/AssociatorState.description: The norm of the three-way associator on shared sites.
interaction/AssociatorState.title: AssociatorState

interaction/AssociatorTriple.description: Three entities whose interaction exhibits non-associativity due to read-write interleaving.
interaction/AssociatorTriple.title: AssociatorTriple

interaction/CommutatorState.description: The norm ‖[δ_A, ι_B]‖ on shared sites. Zero iff operators commute on the shared domain.
interaction/CommutatorState.title: CommutatorState

interaction/InteractionContext.description: Two entities sharing sites through composed operations. Properties: entityA, entityB, sharedSiteMask, commutatorNorm.
interaction/InteractionContext.title: InteractionContext

interaction/MutualModelTrace.description: Sequence of AssociatorStates across interaction steps.
interaction/MutualModelTrace.title: MutualModelTrace

interaction/NegotiationTrace.description: Sequence of CommutatorStates across interaction steps.
interaction/NegotiationTrace.title: NegotiationTrace

interaction/ThreeWaySite.description: A site shared by all three entities in an AssociatorTriple.
interaction/ThreeWaySite.title: ThreeWaySite

linear/AffineSite.description: A site that may be pinned at most once (but need not be pinned). Relaxation of LinearSite for incomplete resolution paths.
linear/AffineSite.title: AffineSite

linear/LeaseAllocation.description: A binding between a state:ContextLease and a subset of LinearSites. Formalizes what resources a lease claims.
linear/LeaseAllocation.title: LeaseAllocation

linear/LinearBudget.description: The multiset of LinearSites available at a given point in resolution. Starts as the full site budget; each LinearEffect removes exactly one element.
linear/LinearBudget.title: LinearBudget

linear/LinearEffect.description: A PinningEffect that consumes its target LinearSite. After application, the site is no longer available for pinning by any subsequent effect.
linear/LinearEffect.title: LinearEffect

linear/LinearSite.description: A site index annotated with a linearity constraint: must be pinned exactly once in any complete resolution path.
linear/LinearSite.title: LinearSite

linear/LinearTrace.description: A computation trace where every site in the budget is targeted by exactly one LinearEffect.
linear/LinearTrace.title: LinearTrace

monoidal/MonoidalAssociator.description: The witness that (A⊗B)⊗C ≅ A⊗(B⊗C). The associativity isomorphism.
monoidal/MonoidalAssociator.title: MonoidalAssociator

monoidal/MonoidalProduct.description: A ⊗ B: the sequential composition of two computations. Output of A feeds input of B.
monoidal/MonoidalProduct.title: MonoidalProduct

monoidal/MonoidalUnit.description: The identity computation I: passes input through unchanged. I ⊗ A ≅ A ≅ A ⊗ I.
monoidal/MonoidalUnit.title: MonoidalUnit

morphism/Action.description: The mechanism by which a group applies transforms systematically to a set. Each group element induces a transform of the set. The dihedral action on type space is an action by isometries — every element of D_{2^n} produces an isometric transform of 𝒯_n.
morphism/Action.title: Action

morphism/ApplicationMorphism.description: A transform that applies a ComputationDatum to an input datum, producing an output datum. The output inherits the certificate of the ComputationDatum.
morphism/ApplicationMorphism.title: ApplicationMorphism

morphism/Composition.description: A transform formed by composing two or more transforms sequentially. The categorical composition operation that turns transforms into a category.
morphism/Composition.title: Composition

morphism/CompositionLaw.description: A law governing how operations compose. Records whether the composition is associative, commutative, and what the result operation is. The critical composition law (neg ∘ bnot = succ) is the foundational instance.
morphism/CompositionLaw.title: CompositionLaw

morphism/ComputationDatum.description: A datum whose ring value is the content address of a cert:TransformCertificate. Represents a certified computation as a first-class value within the ring.
morphism/ComputationDatum.title: ComputationDatum

morphism/Embedding.description: An injective, structure-preserving transform across quantum levels. The canonical instance is the level embedding ι : R_n → R_{n'} (n < n'), which preserves addition, multiplication, and content addressing.
morphism/Embedding.title: Embedding

morphism/GroundingCertificate.description: A certificate attesting that a specific grounding round-trip (P∘Π∘G) satisfied the shared-frame condition and landed in the type-equivalent neighbourhood of the source symbol. Witnesses the Surface Symmetry Theorem (op:surfaceSymmetry) for one specific symbol instance.
morphism/GroundingCertificate.title: GroundingCertificate

morphism/GroundingMap.description: A Transform mapping a surface symbol (any schema:Literal) to its ring datum (a schema:Datum) via the content-addressing bijection. Typed, derivation-witnessed, constraint-preserving map from surface to coordinate. Applies identically across NLP tokens, ARC-AGI grid cells, MIDI notes, pixels, sensor readings, and logical propositions.
morphism/GroundingMap.title: GroundingMap

morphism/GroundingWitness.description: One specific surface symbol mapped to one specific grounded address by some GroundingMap.
morphism/GroundingWitness.title: GroundingWitness

morphism/Identity.description: The identity transform on a type: maps every element to itself. The categorical identity morphism.
morphism/Identity.title: Identity

morphism/Isometry.description: A transform that preserves metric structure with respect to a specified metric. In UOR, isometry is metric-relative: neg is a ring isometry, bnot is a Hamming isometry. A transform can be an isometry with respect to one metric but not the other. This is what cert:IsometryCertificate certifies.
morphism/Isometry.title: Isometry

morphism/PartialApplication.description: A ComputationDatum formed by fixing some but not all inputs of a multi-argument transform.
morphism/PartialApplication.title: PartialApplication

morphism/ProjectionMap.description: A Transform mapping a resolved partition:Partition (address neighbourhood) to an ordered sequence of surface symbols. Output ordering determined by the active state:Frame — the same constraint frame that decomposed the input symbol sequence.
morphism/ProjectionMap.title: ProjectionMap

morphism/ProjectionWitness.description: One specific input partition mapped to one specific surface symbol sequence by some ProjectionMap.
morphism/ProjectionWitness.title: ProjectionWitness

morphism/SequenceElement.description: One position in a SymbolSequence: a reified (value, index) pair.
morphism/SequenceElement.title: SequenceElement

morphism/SymbolSequence.description: An ordered sequence of surface symbols. Elements are reified as SequenceElement individuals with explicit position indices.
morphism/SymbolSequence.title: SymbolSequence

morphism/TopologicalDelta.description: A topological delta: records changes in topological invariants (Betti numbers, Euler characteristic, nerve structure) before and after a morphism.
morphism/TopologicalDelta.title: TopologicalDelta

morphism/Transform.description: A map between UOR objects. The root abstraction: source, target, and optionally what structure (if any) is preserved. This is what cert:TransformCertificate certifies.
morphism/Transform.title: Transform

morphism/TransformComposition.description: A ComputationDatum representing the composition f ∘ g of two ComputationDatums. Certified iff both components are certified and range(g) = domain(f).
morphism/TransformComposition.title: TransformComposition

morphism/Witness.description: Abstract supertype for one specific input/output pair witnessing a Transform.
morphism/Witness.title: Witness

observable/AchievabilityStatus.description: The achievability classification of a topological signature in the morphospace. Either Achievable or Forbidden (witnessed by ImpossibilityWitness).
observable/AchievabilityStatus.title: AchievabilityStatus

observable/AxisProjectionObservable.description: ADR-038: an observable whose value is the axis-realized projection of typed sites through an application-declared AxisTuple kernel per ADR-030. Distinct from the seven internally-derived Observable categories (Stratum, Metric, Path, Reduction, Catastrophe, Curvature, Holonomy) — its values carry from the substrate-extension surface (axis kernels), not from the framework's internal algebraic / topological structure. The closed-catalog discipline holds: foundation owns the subclass; applications consume catalog variants through canonical-string-form `args_repr` on `ConstraintRef::Bound`. The args_repr encoding (per ADR-038) is `axis_address=<hex>;kernel=<symbolic>;sites=<site-list>[;target=<target-spec>]` — axis identification by content-address (AXIS_ADDRESS per ADR-030), not by tuple position, so the encoding is application-invariant.
observable/AxisProjectionObservable.title: AxisProjectionObservable

observable/BaseMetric.description: Superclass for the six universal measurements. Every computation on the ring produces these six quantities: d_Δ, σ, J_k, β_k, χ, r.
observable/BaseMetric.title: BaseMetric

observable/BettiNumber.description: The rank of a homology group of the constraint nerve. β_k = rank(H_k(N(C))) counts the k-dimensional holes in the constraint configuration.
observable/BettiNumber.title: BettiNumber

observable/CatastropheCount.description: The number of catastrophe events (qualitative partition changes) in a computation.
observable/CatastropheCount.title: CatastropheCount

observable/CatastropheObservable.description: An observable measuring catastrophe-theoretic properties: thresholds at which qualitative changes occur in the partition.
observable/CatastropheObservable.title: CatastropheObservable

observable/CatastropheThreshold.description: A critical value at which a qualitative change occurs in the partition structure.
observable/CatastropheThreshold.title: CatastropheThreshold

observable/ClosedConstraintPath.description: A sequence of constraint applications forming a closed loop in the constraint nerve — beginning and ending at the same site assignment. The Monodromy of the loop is the net DihedralElement accumulated when traversing it.
observable/ClosedConstraintPath.title: ClosedConstraintPath

observable/Commutator.description: The commutator [f, g](x) = f(g(x)) - g(f(x)) of two operations, measuring their non-commutativity.
observable/Commutator.title: Commutator

observable/CurvatureFlux.description: The integrated curvature over a region of type space: the total metric incompatibility accumulated.
observable/CurvatureFlux.title: CurvatureFlux

observable/CurvatureObservable.description: An observable measuring the curvature of the UOR geometry: the gap between ring-isometry and Hamming-isometry for a given transform.
observable/CurvatureObservable.title: CurvatureObservable

observable/DihedralElement.description: An element of the dihedral group D_{2^n} acting on the type space. Each dihedral element induces an isometry of 𝒯_n.
observable/DihedralElement.title: DihedralElement

observable/EulerCharacteristicObservable.description: The Euler characteristic χ = Σ(−1)^k β_k of the constraint nerve. An integer-valued topological invariant.
observable/EulerCharacteristicObservable.title: EulerCharacteristicObservable

observable/GroundingObservable.description: The grounding metric σ = pinned sites / total sites. Ranges from 0 (no sites pinned) to 1 (fully grounded).
observable/GroundingObservable.title: GroundingObservable

observable/GroundingSigma.description: Observes the grounding completion ratio σ ∈ [0, 1] of a context, where σ = 1 indicates the ground state (state:GroundedContext). Backs the sigma_metric BaseMetric accessor on Grounded<T>.
observable/GroundingSigma.title: GroundingSigma

observable/HammingMetric.description: Distance between two ring elements under the Hamming metric: the number of bit positions where they differ.
observable/HammingMetric.title: HammingMetric

observable/HigherMonodromy.description: The image of πk(N(C)) → Aut(sitek) for k > 1. Generalises the MN_6 monodromy homomorphism.
observable/HigherMonodromy.title: HigherMonodromy

observable/HolonomyGroup.description: The holonomy group of a ConstrainedType: the group of all Monodromy elements achievable by closed paths in the constraint nerve. Always a subgroup of D_{2^n}. Trivial iff the type has trivial monodromy everywhere; equals D_{2^n} iff paths involving both neg and bnot involutions are present.
observable/HolonomyGroup.title: HolonomyGroup

observable/HolonomyObservable.description: An observable measuring holonomy: the accumulated transformation when traversing a closed path in the ring.
observable/HolonomyObservable.title: HolonomyObservable

observable/HomotopyGroup.description: The k-th homotopy group πk(N(C), v) of the constraint nerve based at vertex v.
observable/HomotopyGroup.title: HomotopyGroup

observable/IncompatibilityMetric.description: The metric incompatibility between two ring elements: the divergence between their ring-metric and Hamming-metric distances, measuring geometric curvature.
observable/IncompatibilityMetric.title: IncompatibilityMetric

observable/Jacobian.description: Site-by-site curvature decomposition. J_k measures the discrete derivative of the incompatibility metric at site position k: J_k = |d_R(x, succ(x)) - d_H(x, succ(x))| restricted to position k.
observable/Jacobian.title: Jacobian

observable/JacobianObservable.description: Observes the per-site Jacobian row of a Datum at a particular WittLevel, computed as the sequence of partial derivatives of the ring operation with respect to each site coordinate. Backs the jacobian_metric BaseMetric accessor on Grounded<T>; the Rust-side JacobianMetric<L> is parametric over the level marker.
observable/JacobianObservable.title: JacobianObservable

observable/LandauerBudget.description: A sealed observable carrier for accumulated Landauer cost in nats. Monotonic within a single pipeline invocation. The UOR ring operates at the Landauer temperature (β* = ln 2), so this observable is a direct measure of irreversible bit-erasure performed by the computation up to the witness it accompanies.
observable/LandauerBudget.title: LandauerBudget

observable/LandauerCost.description: The minimum thermodynamic cost (in units of k_B T ln 2) of erasing one bit of site uncertainty. The UOR ring operates at β* = ln 2 — the Landauer temperature.
observable/LandauerCost.title: LandauerCost

observable/LiftObstructionClass.description: The cohomology class in H^2(N(C(T))) representing the LiftObstruction for a specific WittLift. The class is zero iff the obstruction is trivial. When non-zero, it indexes the specific site pair at Q_{n+1} that cannot be closed by the lifted constraint set alone.
observable/LiftObstructionClass.title: LiftObstructionClass

observable/MeasurementUnit.description: A unit of measurement for observable quantities. Each MeasurementUnit individual names a specific unit (bits, ring steps, dimensionless) replacing the string-valued observable:unit property.
observable/MeasurementUnit.title: MeasurementUnit

observable/MetricObservable.description: An observable measuring geometric distance between ring elements under a specific metric.
observable/MetricObservable.title: MetricObservable

observable/Monodromy.description: The monodromy of a closed path: the net transformation accumulated when traversing a loop in the type space.
observable/Monodromy.title: Monodromy

observable/MonodromyClass.description: A classification of a type's holonomy: the subgroup of D_{2^n} generated by all Monodromy observables computed over closed paths in the type's constraint nerve. Trivial iff every closed constraint path returns to its starting site assignment without net dihedral transformation.
observable/MonodromyClass.title: MonodromyClass

observable/Observable.description: A measurable quantity in the UOR Framework. All observables are kernel-computed and user-consumed.
observable/Observable.title: Observable

observable/ParallelTransport.description: The parallel transport of a vector along a path: the canonical lift of the path to the tangent bundle of the ring.
observable/ParallelTransport.title: ParallelTransport

observable/PathLength.description: The length of a path through the ring, measured in operation steps.
observable/PathLength.title: PathLength

observable/PathObservable.description: An observable measuring properties of paths through the ring: path length, total variation, winding number.
observable/PathObservable.title: PathObservable

observable/PhaseBoundaryType.description: A classification of phase boundary in the catastrophe diagram: period boundary (g divides 2^n − 1) or power-of-two boundary (g = 2^k).
observable/PhaseBoundaryType.title: PhaseBoundaryType

observable/ReductionCount.description: The number of distinct reduction sequences in a computation.
observable/ReductionCount.title: ReductionCount

observable/ReductionEntropy.description: The Shannon entropy of the reduction distribution P(j) = 2^{−j}. At the Landauer temperature, this equals ln 2 per reduction step — each step erases exactly one bit of site uncertainty.
observable/ReductionEntropy.title: ReductionEntropy

observable/ReductionLength.description: The number of operation applications in a reduction sequence.
observable/ReductionLength.title: ReductionLength

observable/ReductionObservable.description: An observable measuring reduction properties: the length and count of operation sequences.
observable/ReductionObservable.title: ReductionObservable

observable/ResidualEntropy.description: S_residual: the residual Shannon entropy of the site distribution after partial resolution. Computed as S = (Σ κ_k − χ(N(C))) × ln 2 (IT_7b). Unit: Nats.
observable/ResidualEntropy.title: ResidualEntropy

observable/RingMetric.description: Distance between two ring elements under the ring metric: d_R(x, y) = |x - y| mod 2^n.
observable/RingMetric.title: RingMetric

observable/SpectralGap.description: The smallest positive eigenvalue of the constraint nerve Laplacian. Controls the convergence rate of iterative resolution: larger gap = faster convergence.
observable/SpectralGap.title: SpectralGap

observable/SpectralObservable.description: ADR-049: an observable whose value is a structural reading of a digest's frequency-domain spectrum. Distinct from the seven internally-derived Observable categories (Stratum / Metric / Path / Reduction / Catastrophe / Curvature / Holonomy) — its values are Walsh–Hadamard parities at specific frequencies, not derivable from the framework's internal algebraic/topological structure. Foundation's typed observable `WalshHadamardParity` per ADR-049 falls under this subclass; predicates over its values enter the typed-commitment surface per ADR-048 as `SingletonCommitment<WalshHadamardParity>` operands.
observable/SpectralObservable.title: SpectralObservable

observable/SpectralSequencePage.description: A single page E_r of the quantum level spectral sequence. Carries the page index r and the differential d_r. The sequence converges when all differentials vanish — typically by E_3 for simple constraint configurations.
observable/SpectralSequencePage.title: SpectralSequencePage

observable/StratificationRecord.description: A record of the holonomy stratification of the moduli space at a given quantum level: the list of HolonomyStrata, their codimensions, and their relationship to the MorphospaceBoundary.
observable/StratificationRecord.title: StratificationRecord

observable/StratumDelta.description: The difference in stratum between two ring elements.
observable/StratumDelta.title: StratumDelta

observable/StratumObservable.description: An observable measuring stratum-level properties: position within the ring's layer structure.
observable/StratumObservable.title: StratumObservable

observable/StratumTrajectory.description: The sequence of strata traversed by a path through the ring.
observable/StratumTrajectory.title: StratumTrajectory

observable/StratumValue.description: The stratum index of a ring element.
observable/StratumValue.title: StratumValue

observable/SynthesisSignature.description: A named topological signature: a pair (realised Euler characteristic, realised Betti profile). Linked from TypeSynthesisResult. Allows comparison between the goal signature and the actually achieved signature.
observable/SynthesisSignature.title: SynthesisSignature

observable/ThermoObservable.description: An observable measuring thermodynamic properties of the resolution process: residual entropy, Landauer cost, and reduction distribution statistics.
observable/ThermoObservable.title: ThermoObservable

observable/TopologicalObservable.description: An observable measuring a topological invariant of the resolution space. Topological observables are invariant under continuous deformations of the constraint configuration.
observable/TopologicalObservable.title: TopologicalObservable

observable/TotalVariation.description: The total variation of a path: the sum of metric distances between consecutive elements.
observable/TotalVariation.title: TotalVariation

observable/ValueModObservable.description: Observes a Datum's value modulo a configurable modulus. Used as the bound observable for BoundConstraint instances representing residue and affine constraint kinds (residueConstraintKind, affineConstraintKind).
observable/ValueModObservable.title: ValueModObservable

observable/ValueThresholdObservable.description: ADR-040 + ADR-049: an observable whose value is a byte-sequence threshold comparison reading of a digest. Distinct from the seven internally-derived Observable categories (Stratum / Metric / Path / Reduction / Catastrophe / Curvature / Holonomy) and from SpectralObservable / AxisProjectionObservable — its values carry from `(digest as big-endian unsigned integer) <= (target as big-endian unsigned integer)`, the predicate form ADR-040 named when it committed `type:LexicographicLessEqBound`. Foundation's typed observable `LexicographicLessEqThreshold` per ADR-049 falls under this subclass; the canonical search-cost commitment alias `TargetCommitment = SingletonCommitment<LexicographicLessEqThreshold>` per ADR-048 consumes it. The ConstraintRef::Bound.args_repr canonical-string-form encoding for ValueThresholdObservable arguments carries the target byte sequence as the bound's argument directly.
observable/ValueThresholdObservable.title: ValueThresholdObservable

observable/WhiteheadProduct.description: The Whitehead product [α, β] ∈ πp+q−1 for α ∈ πp, β ∈ πq.
observable/WhiteheadProduct.title: WhiteheadProduct

observable/WindingNumber.description: The winding number of a closed path: the number of times the path wraps around the ring.
observable/WindingNumber.title: WindingNumber

op/AccumulationOperation.description: α: Binding × Context → Context. Non-commutative, associative at convergence (SR_10), arity 2.
op/AccumulationOperation.title: AccumulationOperation

op/BinaryOp.description: A binary operation on the ring: takes two datums and produces one datum.
op/BinaryOp.title: BinaryOp

op/ComposedOperation.description: An operation formed by composing ring operations, witnessed by op:composedOf and morphism/CompositionLaw.
op/ComposedOperation.title: ComposedOperation

op/DihedralGroup.description: The dihedral group D_{2^n} of order 2^(n+1), generated by the ring reflection (neg) and the hypercube reflection (bnot). This group governs the symmetry of the UOR type space.
op/DihedralGroup.title: DihedralGroup

op/DispatchOperation.description: δ: Query × ResolverRegistry → Resolver. Non-commutative, non-associative, arity 2.
op/DispatchOperation.title: DispatchOperation

op/GeometricCharacter.description: The geometric role of a ring operation in the UOR dual-geometry (ring + hypercube). Every op:Operation individual references exactly one GeometricCharacter via op:hasGeometricCharacter. The nine canonical individuals correspond to the action types of the dihedral group D_{2^n}.
op/GeometricCharacter.title: GeometricCharacter

op/Group.description: A group: a set with an associative binary operation, an identity element, and inverses for every element.
op/Group.title: Group

op/GroupPresentation.description: A structured group presentation: generators and relations as typed data rather than prose strings.
op/GroupPresentation.title: GroupPresentation

op/Identity.description: An algebraic identity: a statement that two expressions are equal for all inputs. The critical identity is neg(bnot(x)) = succ(x) for all x in R_n.
op/Identity.title: Identity

op/InferenceOperation.description: ι = P ∘ Π ∘ G (the φ-pipeline composed). Non-commutative, non-associative, arity 2.
op/InferenceOperation.title: InferenceOperation

op/Involution.description: A unary operation f such that f(f(x)) = x for all x in R_n. The two UOR involutions are neg (ring reflection) and bnot (hypercube reflection).
op/Involution.title: Involution

op/LeasePartitionOperation.description: λ: SharedContext × ℕ → ContextLease^k. Non-commutative, non-associative, arity 2.
op/LeasePartitionOperation.title: LeasePartitionOperation

op/Operation.description: An operation on the ring Z/(2^n)Z. The root class for all UOR kernel operations.
op/Operation.title: Operation

op/QuantumThermodynamicDomain.description: A verification domain at the intersection of quantum superposition and classical thermodynamics. Identities in this domain require both SuperpositionDomain and Thermodynamic reasoning simultaneously.
op/QuantumThermodynamicDomain.title: QuantumThermodynamicDomain

op/SessionCompositionOperation.description: κ: Session × Session → Session. Commutative (disjoint leases), associative (SR_8), arity 2.
op/SessionCompositionOperation.title: SessionCompositionOperation

op/UnaryOp.description: A unary operation on the ring: takes one datum and produces one datum.
op/UnaryOp.title: UnaryOp

op/ValidityScopeKind.description: Root class for validity scope individuals. Instances are the four named scope kinds: Universal, ParametricLower, ParametricRange, and LevelSpecific.
op/ValidityScopeKind.title: ValidityScopeKind

op/VerificationDomain.description: A named mathematical discipline through which an algebraic identity is established and grounded. Every op:Identity individual references at least one VerificationDomain via op:verificationDomain. The nine canonical domain individuals are kernel-level constants defined in op/.
op/VerificationDomain.title: VerificationDomain

op/WittLevelBinding.description: A record linking an op:Identity individual to a specific quantum level at which it has been verified. Non-functional: one WittLevelBinding per (Identity, WittLevel) pair verified.
op/WittLevelBinding.title: WittLevelBinding

operad/OperadComposition.description: A specific nesting: outer type F applied to inner type G.
operad/OperadComposition.title: OperadComposition

operad/StructuralOperad.description: The composition structure on the eight structural types. Governs how types nest.
operad/StructuralOperad.title: StructuralOperad

parallel/DisjointnessCertificate.description: A kernel-produced certificate attesting that the site targets of two computations are disjoint.
parallel/DisjointnessCertificate.title: DisjointnessCertificate

parallel/ParallelProduct.description: A ∥ B: two computations with provably disjoint site targets. Execution order does not affect the result.
parallel/ParallelProduct.title: ParallelProduct

parallel/ParallelTrace.description: A computation trace recording interleaved steps from two parallel computations. Valid iff every interleaving produces the same final context.
parallel/ParallelTrace.title: ParallelTrace

parallel/SitePartitioning.description: A partition of the total site budget into disjoint subsets, one per parallel component. The sum of subset cardinalities equals the total site budget.
parallel/SitePartitioning.title: SitePartitioning

parallel/SynchronizationPoint.description: A point where two parallel computations must agree on shared state before proceeding. Only applicable when parallelism is partial.
parallel/SynchronizationPoint.title: SynchronizationPoint

partition/CartesianPartitionProduct.description: The Cartesian product of partitions. Classifies the nerve topology of A ⊠ B as a simplicial product (χ multiplicative per CPT_3, Betti by Künneth per CPT_4) rather than a site-disjoint union (χ additive — PartitionProduct). Site budget is |S_A| + |S_B| per CPT_1 — the bit width of the product state space. Partition-ness is asserted via leftCartesianFactor / rightCartesianFactor (both ranged at Partition), matching the sibling pattern for PartitionProduct and PartitionCoproduct. Satisfies CPT_1–CPT_6 per this amendment.
partition/CartesianPartitionProduct.title: CartesianPartitionProduct

partition/Complement.description: Elements of R_n that fall outside the active carrier — i.e., outside the type's domain. These are ring elements that do not participate in the current type resolution.
partition/Complement.title: Complement

partition/Component.description: A single component of a partition: a set of datum values belonging to one of the four categories.
partition/Component.title: Component

partition/FreeRank.description: The site budget for a partition: an accounting of how many sites are pinned (determined by constraints) versus free (still available for further refinement). A closed budget means all sites are pinned and the type is fully resolved.
partition/FreeRank.title: FreeRank

partition/FreeRankObservable.description: Observes the free-rank of the partition associated with a Datum's site context, recording the count of unbound sites at the moment of observation. Used as the bound observable for the siteConstraintKind BoundConstraint.
partition/FreeRankObservable.title: FreeRankObservable

partition/IrreducibleSet.description: The set of irreducible elements under the active type: elements whose only factorizations involve units or themselves. Analogous to prime elements in a ring.
partition/IrreducibleSet.title: IrreducibleSet

partition/Partition.description: A four-component partition of R_n produced by resolving a type declaration. The four components — Irreducible, Reducible, Units, Exterior — are mutually disjoint and exhaustive over the carrier.
partition/Partition.title: Partition

partition/PartitionComponent.description: Closed enumeration of partition component kinds: Irreducible (non-factorizable), Reducible (factorizable into non-trivial parts), Units (invertible), Exterior (outside the factorization domain). Codegen treats this as an enum class with exactly 4 individuals.
partition/PartitionComponent.title: PartitionComponent

partition/PartitionCoproduct.description: The coproduct (disjoint union) of two partitions: partition(A + B) = partition(A) ⊕ partition(B). The four-component structure combines via disjoint union under the sum type construction (PT_2b). Carries leftSummand and rightSummand links to the operand partitions.
partition/PartitionCoproduct.title: PartitionCoproduct

partition/PartitionProduct.description: The tensor product of two partitions: partition(A × B) = partition(A) ⊗ partition(B). The four-component structure combines component-wise under the product type construction (PT_2a). Carries leftFactor and rightFactor links to the operand partitions.
partition/PartitionProduct.title: PartitionProduct

partition/ReducibleSet.description: The set of reducible non-unit elements: elements that can be expressed as a product of two or more non-unit elements.
partition/ReducibleSet.title: ReducibleSet

partition/SiteBinding.description: A record of a single site being pinned by a constraint. Links a specific site coordinate to the constraint that determined its value.
partition/SiteBinding.title: SiteBinding

partition/SiteIndex.description: A single site coordinate in the iterated Z/2Z fibration. Each site represents one binary degree of freedom in the ring's structure. The total number of sites equals the quantum level n.
partition/SiteIndex.title: SiteIndex

partition/TagSite.description: The distinguishing site in a PartitionCoproduct whose value (0 or 1) selects the variant. Logically, the tag is not a data site of either operand (ST_6) and carries exactly the ln 2 entropy quantum (ST_2). Its physical placement in a flat constraint layout follows the foundation layout convention: layoutTagSite = max(SITE_COUNT(A), SITE_COUNT(B)), so the tag does not collide with any inherited bookkeeping sites when operands are themselves coproducts.
partition/TagSite.title: TagSite

partition/UnitGroup.description: The set of invertible elements (units) in the carrier: elements with a multiplicative inverse. In Z/(2^n)Z, the units are the odd integers.
partition/UnitGroup.title: UnitGroup

predicate/DispatchRule.description: A pair (Predicate, Target) where Target is a resolver:Resolver class. The kernel evaluates the predicate; if true, the target resolver is selected.
predicate/DispatchRule.title: DispatchRule

predicate/DispatchTable.description: An ordered set of DispatchRules for a single dispatch point. Must satisfy exhaustiveness and mutual exclusion.
predicate/DispatchTable.title: DispatchTable

predicate/GuardedTransition.description: A triple (StatePredicate, effect:Effect, reduction:ReductionStep). The guard is a StatePredicate; if true, the effect is applied and the reduction advances to the target step.
predicate/GuardedTransition.title: GuardedTransition

predicate/MatchArm.description: A single case in a pattern match: a Predicate and a result Term. The match evaluates predicates in order and returns the result of the first matching arm.
predicate/MatchArm.title: MatchArm

predicate/MatchExpression.description: A term formed by evaluating a sequence of MatchArms. Extends the term language with deterministic conditional evaluation.
predicate/MatchExpression.title: MatchExpression

predicate/Predicate.description: A total, pure, boolean-valued function on a kernel object. Evaluation terminates for all inputs and produces no side effects.
predicate/Predicate.title: Predicate

predicate/SitePredicate.description: A predicate over partition:SiteIndex. Used for site-level selection in geodesic resolution.
predicate/SitePredicate.title: SitePredicate

predicate/StatePredicate.description: A predicate over state:Context or reduction:ReductionState. Used for reduction step guards.
predicate/StatePredicate.title: StatePredicate

predicate/TypePredicate.description: A predicate over type:TypeDefinition. Used for resolver dispatch.
predicate/TypePredicate.title: TypePredicate

proof/AxiomaticDerivation.description: A proof that follows from previously established axioms or definitions by equational, structural, or topological reasoning. The proof:derivationWitness property links to a derivation:Derivation individual recording the rewrite chain. All pipeline, constraint, observable, and topological identities are AxiomaticDerivations.
proof/AxiomaticDerivation.title: AxiomaticDerivation

proof/CoherenceProof.description: A proof of coherence: the type system and ring structure are mutually consistent at a given quantum level.
proof/CoherenceProof.title: CoherenceProof

proof/ComputationCertificate.description: A proof confirmed by exhaustive execution over R_n at a specific quantum level. The kernel ran the identity against all 2^n inputs and observed that it holds. The proof:atWittLevel property records the level; proof:witness links to the WitnessData. CriticalIdentityProof is a subclass of ComputationCertificate.
proof/ComputationCertificate.title: ComputationCertificate

proof/ComputationStep.description: A proof step performing exhaustive computation at a specific quantum level as verification witness.
proof/ComputationStep.title: ComputationStep

proof/CriticalIdentityProof.description: A proof of the critical identity: neg(bnot(x)) = succ(x) for all x in R_n. This is the foundational theorem of the UOR kernel.
proof/CriticalIdentityProof.title: CriticalIdentityProof

proof/DerivationTerm.description: Root AST node for proof construction terms. Distinct from schema:TermExpression which represents mathematical terms; DerivationTerm represents proof constructions (tactic applications, lemma invocations, induction scaffolding).
proof/DerivationTerm.title: DerivationTerm

proof/ImpossibilityWitness.description: A formal witness that a topological signature (χ, β_k) is impossible to achieve for any ConstrainedType. Carries the algebraic reason and the verification domain grounding the impossibility.
proof/ImpossibilityWitness.title: ImpossibilityWitness

proof/InductionStep.description: A proof step performing structural induction: base case derivation, inductive hypothesis, and step derivation.
proof/InductionStep.title: InductionStep

proof/InductiveProof.description: A proof by structural induction on the quantum level index k. Carries a base case proof, an inductive step proof, and the minimum k for which the induction holds.
proof/InductiveProof.title: InductiveProof

proof/InhabitanceImpossibilityWitness.description: A specialisation of proof:ImpossibilityWitness produced when the inhabitance search determines that the carrier of a ConstrainedType is empty. Aggregates into the existing proof:MorphospaceBoundary alongside other impossibility witnesses, inheriting its O(1) amortised lookup discipline for previously resolved signatures.
proof/InhabitanceImpossibilityWitness.title: InhabitanceImpossibilityWitness

proof/LemmaInvocation.description: A proof step invoking a previously proved identity as a lemma. References the identity via proof:dependsOn.
proof/LemmaInvocation.title: LemmaInvocation

proof/MorphospaceBoundary.description: An aggregate of ImpossibilityWitness instances forming the queryable morphospace map. SPARQL over this structure answers achievability queries in O(1).
proof/MorphospaceBoundary.title: MorphospaceBoundary

proof/MorphospaceRecord.description: A formal record of a morphospace boundary point — either an achievable or forbidden topological signature. Aggregated by MorphospaceBoundary to form the queryable morphospace map.
proof/MorphospaceRecord.title: MorphospaceRecord

proof/Proof.description: A kernel-produced attestation that a given algebraic property holds. The root class for all proof types.
proof/Proof.title: Proof

proof/ProofStrategy.description: A controlled vocabulary of proof methods. Each proof individual carries exactly one strategy from this vocabulary, enabling compilation to verified theorem provers.
proof/ProofStrategy.title: ProofStrategy

proof/TacticApplication.description: A proof step applying a named tactic (from ProofStrategy) with arguments. Maps to a Lean4 tactic invocation.
proof/TacticApplication.title: TacticApplication

proof/WitnessData.description: Supporting data for a proof: specific examples, counter-examples checked, or intermediate computation results.
proof/WitnessData.title: WitnessData

query/CoordinateQuery.description: A query for the ring-coordinate position of a datum: its stratum, spectrum, and address within the ring geometry.
query/CoordinateQuery.title: CoordinateQuery

query/MetricQuery.description: A query for a metric value between two datums: ring distance, Hamming distance, or their divergence (curvature).
query/MetricQuery.title: MetricQuery

query/Query.description: A request for information from the UOR kernel. The root abstraction for all query types.
query/Query.title: Query

query/RelationQuery.description: A Query with a known source address, a typed relation constraint, and an open target partition:FreeRank. Represents any question of the form: given this source symbol and relation type, what is the target? The relation type is read from the observable coordinate triple (d_R, d_H, d_I) — not externally supplied. Applies across NLP, ARC-AGI grids, music, images, sensor streams, and logical formulae.
query/RelationQuery.title: RelationQuery

query/RepresentationQuery.description: A query for the canonical representation of a datum or term: its normal form under the active resolver strategy.
query/RepresentationQuery.title: RepresentationQuery

query/SessionQuery.description: A RelationQuery that explicitly declares its session membership, allowing the conformance suite to validate session-scoped site reduction across multi-turn Prism deployments.
query/SessionQuery.title: SessionQuery

query/TriadProjection.description: A classification of coordinate types that a CoordinateQuery can extract. Each TriadProjection individual names a specific coordinate system (stratum, spectrum, address) replacing the string-valued query:coordinate property.
query/TriadProjection.title: TriadProjection

recursion/BaseCase.description: The match arm selected when the descent measure reaches zero or the base predicate is satisfied. Produces a direct result without further recursion.
recursion/BaseCase.title: BaseCase

recursion/BoundedRecursion.description: A self-referential computation parameterized by a descent measure. Each recursive step strictly decreases the measure; the computation terminates when the base case predicate is satisfied.
recursion/BoundedRecursion.title: BoundedRecursion

recursion/DescentMeasure.description: A well-founded function from computation state to xsd:nonNegativeInteger. Strictly decreases on every recursive step.
recursion/DescentMeasure.title: DescentMeasure

recursion/RecursionTrace.description: A computation trace recording the sequence of recursive steps, measure values, and the base case result. The trace length is bounded by the initial measure value.
recursion/RecursionTrace.title: RecursionTrace

recursion/RecursiveCase.description: The match arm selected when the descent measure is positive. Applies a decomposition step, decreases the measure, and invokes the recursion.
recursion/RecursiveCase.title: RecursiveCase

recursion/RecursiveStep.description: A single step in a bounded recursion: the decomposition applied before the recursive invocation. Must strictly decrease the DescentMeasure.
recursion/RecursiveStep.title: RecursiveStep

recursion/StructuralRecursion.description: A bounded recursion where the descent measure is the structural size of the input type (site count, constraint count, or operad nesting depth).
recursion/StructuralRecursion.title: StructuralRecursion

reduction/BackPressureSignal.description: Flow control when reduction produces faster than downstream can consume.
reduction/BackPressureSignal.title: BackPressureSignal

reduction/ComparisonPredicate.description: Predicate comparing a state field against a value.
reduction/ComparisonPredicate.title: ComparisonPredicate

reduction/CompileUnit.description: The typed input graph submitted to the reduction pipeline. Packages a root Term, target quantum level, verification domains, and thermodynamic budget. Stage 0 accepts exactly one CompileUnit and initializes the reduction state vector from it.
reduction/CompileUnit.title: CompileUnit

reduction/ComplexConjugateRollback.description: Recovery operation when a phase gate fails: z → z̄. Involutory: applying twice yields the original value.
reduction/ComplexConjugateRollback.title: ComplexConjugateRollback

reduction/DeferredQuerySet.description: Queries postponed to a future epoch.
reduction/DeferredQuerySet.title: DeferredQuerySet

reduction/Epoch.description: Temporal segment of reduction execution. Each epoch represents one complete pass through the reduction stages.
reduction/Epoch.title: Epoch

reduction/EpochBoundary.description: Transition between epochs. Carries metadata about the epoch boundary crossing.
reduction/EpochBoundary.title: EpochBoundary

reduction/EqualsPredicate.description: Predicate testing equality of two expressions.
reduction/EqualsPredicate.title: EqualsPredicate

reduction/EulerReduction.description: The composite endofunctor ψ = ψ_9 ∘ … ∘ ψ_1, parameterized by Ω = e^{iπ/6}.
reduction/EulerReduction.title: EulerReduction

reduction/FailureField.description: An ontology fact describing a single field on a reduction:PipelineFailureReason variant. The v0.2.1 Rust codegen walks FailureField individuals filtered by ofFailure to assemble the PipelineFailure enum's variant fields. Adding a new field to a failure variant requires only adding a new FailureField individual.
reduction/FailureField.title: FailureField

reduction/FeasibilityResult.description: Result of a preflight check: feasibility witness or infeasibility witness.
reduction/FeasibilityResult.title: FeasibilityResult

reduction/GroundingPredicate.description: Predicate testing whether grounding exceeds a threshold.
reduction/GroundingPredicate.title: GroundingPredicate

reduction/GuardExpression.description: A conjunction of PredicateExpressions that must hold for a transition to fire.
reduction/GuardExpression.title: GuardExpression

reduction/LeaseCheckpoint.description: Snapshot of lease state at a point in time.
reduction/LeaseCheckpoint.title: LeaseCheckpoint

reduction/LeaseState.description: Lifecycle of a partitioned context lease: Pending → Active → Released/Expired/Suspended.
reduction/LeaseState.title: LeaseState

reduction/ManagedLease.description: A context lease with lifecycle tracking.
reduction/ManagedLease.title: ManagedLease

reduction/MembershipPredicate.description: Predicate testing membership of an element in a set.
reduction/MembershipPredicate.title: MembershipPredicate

reduction/NonNullPredicate.description: Predicate testing that a field is non-null.
reduction/NonNullPredicate.title: NonNullPredicate

reduction/PhaseGateAttestation.description: Validation at each stage boundary checking that the accumulated phase angle matches the expected Ω^k.
reduction/PhaseGateAttestation.title: PhaseGateAttestation

reduction/PhaseRotationScheduler.description: Schedule Ω⁰, Ω¹, …, Ω⁵ assigning a phase angle to each stage of the reduction.
reduction/PhaseRotationScheduler.title: PhaseRotationScheduler

reduction/PipelineFailureReason.description: Typed failure: DispatchMiss, GroundingFailure, ConvergenceStall, etc.
reduction/PipelineFailureReason.title: PipelineFailureReason

reduction/PredicateExpression.description: A Boolean expression over the reduction state. The atomic guard unit.
reduction/PredicateExpression.title: PredicateExpression

reduction/PreflightCheck.description: A pre-execution validation: feasibility, dispatch coverage, coherence.
reduction/PreflightCheck.title: PreflightCheck

reduction/PropertyBind.description: A single site pinning: target site + value.
reduction/PropertyBind.title: PropertyBind

reduction/QuerySubtypePredicate.description: Predicate testing whether a query is a subtype of a given type.
reduction/QuerySubtypePredicate.title: QuerySubtypePredicate

reduction/ReductionAdvance.description: Advancement from one ReductionStep to the next.
reduction/ReductionAdvance.title: ReductionAdvance

reduction/ReductionRule.description: Guard-effect pair governing stage transitions in the reduction. The guard must be satisfied before the effect is applied.
reduction/ReductionRule.title: ReductionRule

reduction/ReductionState.description: State of reduction execution at a specific point, including the current stage, phase angle, and pinned mask.
reduction/ReductionState.title: ReductionState

reduction/ReductionStep.description: A named stage of the reduction. The standard reduction has six stages (Initialization through Convergence).
reduction/ReductionStep.title: ReductionStep

reduction/ReductionTransaction.description: An atomic group of state changes within the reduction.
reduction/ReductionTransaction.title: ReductionTransaction

reduction/SatBound.description: Declarative bound for SAT-fragment deciders. The v0.2.1 pipeline driver reads these individuals at codegen time to emit the bounded iterative Tarjan SCC (2-SAT) and unit propagation (Horn-SAT) implementations.
reduction/SatBound.title: SatBound

reduction/ServiceWindow.description: A sliding window over recent epochs providing BaseContext.
reduction/ServiceWindow.title: ServiceWindow

reduction/SiteCoveragePredicate.description: Predicate testing whether a site coverage target is met.
reduction/SiteCoveragePredicate.title: SiteCoveragePredicate

reduction/SubleaseTransfer.description: Transfer of a lease from one computation to another.
reduction/SubleaseTransfer.title: SubleaseTransfer

reduction/TargetConvergenceAngle.description: The angle at which the reduction terminates (default: π).
reduction/TargetConvergenceAngle.title: TargetConvergenceAngle

reduction/TimingBound.description: Declarative timing bound for reduction pipeline stages. Values in nanoseconds at the v0.2.1 reference hardware.
reduction/TimingBound.title: TimingBound

reduction/TransitionEffect.description: State changes applied when a transition fires. Contains PropertyBind steps.
reduction/TransitionEffect.title: TransitionEffect

region/AddressRegion.description: A contiguous range of u:Element values accessible during a single reduction step. Defines the resolver’s working set.
region/AddressRegion.title: AddressRegion

region/LocalityMetric.description: A metric on u:Element values determining which addresses are near each other for resolution purposes.
region/LocalityMetric.title: LocalityMetric

region/RegionAllocation.description: An assignment of AddressRegions to reduction steps for a given computation. Enables Prism to pre-compute memory layout.
region/RegionAllocation.title: RegionAllocation

region/RegionBound.description: The boundary of an AddressRegion: a pair (lowerAddress, upperAddress) in the content-address ordering.
region/RegionBound.title: RegionBound

region/WorkingSet.description: The set of AddressRegions needed by a resolver at a specific reduction step for a specific type. Computable from the type’s constraint nerve.
region/WorkingSet.title: WorkingSet

resolver/CanonicalFormResolver.description: Resolves types by computing canonical forms via term rewriting. Applies the critical identity and normalization rules to reduce terms to unique canonical representatives.
resolver/CanonicalFormResolver.title: CanonicalFormResolver

resolver/CechNerve.description: The simplicial complex whose vertices are constraints and where a k-simplex exists iff the corresponding k+1 constraints have nonempty intersection. The nerve's topology governs resolution convergence: trivial homology ↔ smooth convergence, non-trivial homology ↔ potential stalls.
resolver/CechNerve.title: CechNerve

resolver/CertifyMapping.description: An ontology fact recording that a resolver:Resolver subclass produces a specific cert:Certificate subclass on success and a specific proof:ImpossibilityWitness subclass on failure. The v0.2.1 Rust codegen reads CertifyMapping individuals to emit foundation::Certify trait impls for each resolver class, keeping the mapping data-driven rather than hand-tabulated in source.
resolver/CertifyMapping.title: CertifyMapping

resolver/CompletenessResolver.description: A specialisation of Resolver driving the completeness certification loop. Accepts a CompletenessCandidate, runs the ψ-pipeline (reading nerveEulerCharacteristic from ResolutionState), and either issues a CompletenessCertificate or produces a RefinementSuggestion.
resolver/CompletenessResolver.title: CompletenessResolver

resolver/ComplexityClass.description: A computational complexity classification for resolvers. Each resolver's asymptotic runtime is typed as a named ComplexityClass individual rather than a free string.
resolver/ComplexityClass.title: ComplexityClass
